title: Essential Modern Outdoor Square Dining Table
summary: A pocket-holed 4x4-leg outdoor table with a cedar-picket top and a built-in umbrella opening, sized to seat a full table's worth of chairs.
description:
This table's aprons sit inset 1/2" from the legs' outside faces — a small, deliberate reveal that gives the base a genuine post-and-apron look rather than a plain box. Build two matching leg-and-apron end assemblies first, then join them with the remaining aprons to complete the frame before anything else goes on.

The under-table support boards do double duty: they carry the tabletop, and — if you're adding the optional umbrella hole — their spacing determines the size of the opening. Measure your own support spacing carefully before cutting the umbrella-opening blocking, since the cut list numbers for that piece are explicitly given as approximate and dependent on how your own supports land.

Square the frame by comparing diagonal measurements before laying out the cedar picket top, and lay out all the top boards first to get an even overhang on all four sides before committing to fasteners. Sand the cedar top thoroughly, coarse to fine — cedar splinters more readily than most other decking lumber, and a dining table's top gets touched by bare forearms and hands far more than most outdoor furniture.

materials.name: Cedar fence pickets, 5.5" wide, 5' or 6' long

cutList.note: Inset 1/2" from the outside face of the legs.
Measure to ensure a tight fit.
Optional. Measure to fit your own support spacing.

cutList.material: Cedar fence pickets, 5.5" wide, 5' or 6' long
cutList.note: Can run long and trim all ends together after installation.

steps.title: Build the leg-and-apron ends
steps.body: Drill two 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of every 2x4 apron (except the umbrella blocking, if used). Attach aprons to legs, inset 1/2" from the legs' outside face. Build two identical end assemblies.

steps.title: Join the frame
steps.body: Attach the remaining aprons to the leg assemblies to complete the frame.

steps.title: Add the under-table supports
steps.body: Measure and cut the under-table support boards to fit tightly, drill two pocket holes on each end, mark placement, and attach. If adding umbrella support blocking, add it now at the center of the framing.

steps.title: Square the frame and lay the top
steps.body: Adjust the table for square by comparing diagonal measurements between opposite corners until they match. Lay out the cedar picket top boards and adjust for an even overhang on all four sides, then attach with 2" screws.
materials: Cedar fence pickets, 5.5" wide, 5' or 6' long

steps.title: Cut the umbrella opening (optional)
steps.body: If adding umbrella support, cut the opening through the top and blocking with a jigsaw at the center of the framing.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand the cedar top thoroughly, starting with coarse sandpaper and working down to fine — cedar splinters more readily than most decking lumber. Additional corner blocking can be added under each leg joint for extra squareness if desired.

images.alt: Modern outdoor square dining table with a cedar picket top
